findings.description: Bare `except:` clause silently swallows all exceptions including KeyboardInterrupt, SystemExit, and MemoryError. Errors are never logged, making debugging impossible and hiding issues like malformed URLs, DNS failures, authentication errors, or connection timeouts.
findings.evidence: except:\n    pass — no exception type, no logging, no re-raise. Removing this block would cause the program to crash visibly, which is strictly better than silent failure.

findings.description: Retry loop retries on every possible exception indiscriminately. A 404, a permissions error, or a programming bug (TypeError, AttributeError) will be retried 3 times with no backoff, wasting time and obscuring the root cause.
findings.evidence: for attempt in range(3): ... except: pass — no distinction between transient network errors (worth retrying) and permanent errors (not worth retrying). KT1 fails: no contributor should apply retry-everything silently as a pattern.

findings.description: `process_feed` does not handle the case where `output_dir` does not exist, and `output_path.write_text(...)` will raise FileNotFoundError with no surrounding error handling. Combined with F1, upstream errors return None silently, but downstream write errors will surface as unhandled exceptions.
findings.evidence: output_path.write_text(...) called without ensuring output_dir exists. No try/except around write. Inconsistent resilience strategy compared to the fetch path.

clean.location: lines 25-31 (successful fetch path, CSV parsing, row logging)
